jar_support.c

00013  * included at /legal/license.txt).

00021  * Clara, CA 95054 or visit www.sun.com if you need additional
00022  * information or have any questions. 
00023  */
00024 
00025 /*=========================================================================
00026  * SYSTEM:    Verifier
00027  * SUBSYSTEM: JAR support routines for the verifier.
00028  * FILE:      jar_support.c
00029  * OVERVIEW:  JAR support routines for verifying class files from a ZIP or
00030  *            JAR file.
00031  *            Note that the JAR file reader used is based on the KVM
00032  *            implementation with some modifications.
00033  *=======================================================================*/
00034 
00035 /*=========================================================================
00036  * Include files
00037  *=======================================================================*/
00038 
00039 #include "wrappers.h"
00040 
00041 #include "sys_api.h"
00042 #include "path_md.h"
00043 #include "path.h"
00044 #include "oobj.h"
00045 #include "jar.h"
00046 #include "convert_md.h"
00047 
00048 /*=========================================================================
00049  * Globals and extern declarations
00050  *=======================================================================*/
00051 
00052 extern int errno;
00053 char str_buffer[STRINGBUFFERSIZE];   /*  shared string buffer */
00054 bool JARfile = false;    /* if true, indicates that output is in a
00055                                                   JAR file */
00056 extern bool tmpDirExists;
00057                            /* if true, indicates that a temp dir exists
00058                               with classes to be verified */
00059 char *zipFileName = NULL;  /* stores name of the zip file */
00060 extern char tmp_dir[32];   /* temporary directory for storing
00061                                                   verified classes */
00062 extern char *output_dir;   /* output directory */
00063 
00064 char manifestfile[1024];   /* used for saving the JAR manifest file name */
00065 
00066 extern void VerifyFile(register char *fn);
00067 
00068 /*=========================================================================
00069  * FUNCTION:      isJARfile
00070  * OVERVIEW:      Determines if the given file is a JAR or ZIP file.
00071  *                Returns true if the suffix ends with ".jar" or ".zip".
00072  * INTERFACE:
00073  *   parameters:  fn:      name of the JAR file
00074  *                length:  length of data, in bytes
00075  *   returns:     boolean type
00076  *=======================================================================*/
00077 bool
00078 isJARfile (char *fn, int length)
00079 {
00080     char *suffix;
00081 
00082     if (length >= 4 &&
00083        (( suffix = fn + length - 4)[0] == '.') &&
00084          ((( _toupper(suffix[1]) == 'Z') &&
00085           ( _toupper(suffix[2]) == 'I') &&
00086           ( _toupper(suffix[3]) == 'P')) ||
00087          (( _toupper(suffix[1]) == 'J') &&
00088           ( _toupper(suffix[2]) == 'A') &&
00089           ( _toupper(suffix[3]) == 'R')))) {
00090         return true;
00091     } else {
00092         return false;
00093     }
00094 }
00095 
00096 /*=========================================================================
00097  * FUNCTION:      isManifestfile
00098  * OVERVIEW:      Determines if the given file is a JAR Manifest file.
00099  *                Returns true if the file ends with "MANIFEST.MF".
00100  * INTERFACE:
00101  *   parameters:  fn:      name of the JAR manifest file
00102  *                length:  length of data, in bytes
00103  *   returns:     boolean type
00104  *=======================================================================*/
00105 bool
00106 isManifestfile (char *fn, int length)
00107 {
00108     if ((length >= 11) &&
00109        (strcmp(fn + length - 11, "MANIFEST.MF") == 0)) {
00110         return true;
00111     } else {
00112         return false;
00113     }
00114 }
00115 
00116 /*=========================================================================
00117  * FUNCTION:      ensure_tmpdir_exists
00118  * OVERVIEW:      Validates to ensure that the tmpdir exists using the
00119  *                system-specific directory delimiters.
00120  *
00121  * INTERFACE:
00122  *   parameters:  char* dir name
00123  *   returns:     nothing
00124  *=======================================================================*/
00125 void ensure_tmpdir_exists(char *dir)
00126 {
00127     struct stat stat_buf;
00128     char *parent;
00129     char *q;
00130     if (dir[0] == 0) {
00131         return;
00132     }
00133     parent = strdup(dir);
00134     q = strrchr(parent, (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR);
00135     if (q) {
00136         *q = 0;
00137         ensure_tmpdir_exists(parent);
00138     }
00139     if (stat(dir, &stat_buf) < 0) {
00140         if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose) {
00141             jio_fprintf(stderr, "Creating output directory [%s]\n", dir);
00142         }
00143         #if HAVE_MKDIR_ONE_ARGUMENT
00144         mkdir(dir);
00145         #else
00146         mkdir(dir, 0755);
00147         #endif
00148     }
00149     free(parent);
00150 }
00151 
00152 /*=========================================================================
00153  * FUNCTION:      JARname2fname
00154  * OVERVIEW:      Converts JAR name to the system-specific file name with
00155  *                the correct directory delimiters.
00156  *
00157  * INTERFACE:
00158  *   parameters:  char* source JAR name
00159  *                char* dest file name
00160  *                int size
00161  *   returns:     char*
00162  *=======================================================================*/
00163 char*
00164 JARname2fname(char *src, char *dst, int size) {
00165     char *buf = dst;
00166     for (; (--size > 0) && (*src != '\0') ; src++, dst++) {
00167     if (*src == '/') {
00168         *dst = (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR;
00169     }  else {
00170         *dst = *src;
00171     }
00172     }
00173     dst++;
00174     *dst = '\0';
00175     return buf;
00176 }
00177 
00178 /*=========================================================================
00179  * FUNCTION:      getZipEntry
00180  * OVERVIEW:      Converts a zip file to a Zip entry type.
00181  * INTERFACE:
00182  *   parameters:  zipFile:      name of the JAR file
00183  *                len:          length of data, in bytes
00184  *   returns:     zip entry type
00185  *=======================================================================*/
00186 zip_t *
00187 getZipEntry (char *zipFile, int len) {
00188     zip_t * zipEntry = NULL;  /* for processing errors */
00189 
00190     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00191         jio_fprintf(stderr,
00192             "getZipEntry: JAR file [%s] Size [%d]\n", zipFile, len);
00193 
00194     /* create the zip entry for loading the ZIP file */
00195     zipEntry = (zip_t *) sysMalloc(sizeof(zip_t) + len);
00196     if (zipEntry == NULL) {
00197         fprintf(stderr, "getZipEntry: Out of memory\n");
00198         exit(1);
00199     }
00200 
00201     memcpy(zipEntry->name, zipFile, len);
00202 
00203     zipEntry->name[len] = '\0';
00204 
00205     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00206         jio_fprintf(stderr, "getZipEntry: Zip Entry Name [%s]\n", zipEntry->name);
00207 
00208     zipEntry->type = '\0';
00209     return zipEntry;
00210 }
00211 
00212 /*=========================================================================
00213  * FUNCTION:      findJARDirectories
00214  * OVERVIEW:      Helper function used for JAR loading for locating JAR
00215  *                directories.
00216  *                It returns TRUE if it is successful in locating the
00217  *                JAR directory headers, false otherwise.
00218  *                If successful,
00219  *                entry->jar.locpos is set to the position of the first
00220  *                local header.
00221  *                entry->jar.cenpos is set to the position of the first
00222  *                central header.
00223  *
00224  *                Note that *locpos pointer is the logical "0" of the file.
00225  *                All offsets extracted need to have this value added to them.
00226  *
00227  * INTERFACE:
00228  *   parameters:  entry:     zipFileEntry
00229  *                statbuf:   pointer to the stat buffer
00230  *   returns:     boolean type
00231  *=======================================================================*/
00232 
00233 bool
00234 findJARDirectories(zip_t *entry, struct stat *statbuf)
00235 {
00236     bool result = false;
00237     long length = statbuf->st_size;
00238     long position, minPosition;
00239     char *bp;
00240     FILE *file;
00241 
00242     char *buffer = str_buffer;
00243     unsigned const int bufferSize = STRINGBUFFERSIZE;
00244 
00245     /* Calculate the smallest possible position for the end header.  It
00246      * can be at most 0xFFFF + ENDHDRSIZ bytes from the end of the file, but
00247      * the file must also have a local header and a central header
00248      */
00249     minPosition = length - (0xFFFF + ENDHDRSIZ);
00250     if (minPosition < LOCHDRSIZ + CENHDRSIZ) {
00251         minPosition = LOCHDRSIZ + CENHDRSIZ;
00252     }
00253 
00254     file = fopen(entry->name, "rb");
00255     if (file == NULL) {
00256         goto done;
00257     }
00258 
00259     /* Read in the last ENDHDRSIZ bytes into the buffer.  99% of the time,
00260      * the file won't have a comment, and this is the only read we'll need */
00261     if (   (fseek(file, -ENDHDRSIZ, SEEK_END) < 0)
00262         || (fread(buffer, sizeof(char), ENDHDRSIZ, file) != ENDHDRSIZ)) {
00263         goto done;
00264     }
00265     /* Get the position in the file stored into buffer[0] */
00266     position = length - ENDHDRSIZ;  /* Position in file of buffer[0] */
00267     bp = buffer;            /* Where to start looking */
00268     for (;;) {
00269         /* "buffer" contains a block of data from the file, starting at
00270          * position "position" in the file.
00271          * We investigate whether   position + (bp - buffer)  is the start
00272          * of the end header in the zip file.  This file position is at
00273          * position bp in the buffer.
00274          */
00275         /* Use simplified version of Knuth Morris Pratt search algorithm. */
00276         switch(bp[0]) {
00277             case '\006':   /* The header must start at least 3 bytes back */
00278                 bp -= 3; break;
00279             case '\005':   /* The header must start at least 2 bytes back  */
00280                 bp -= 2; break;
00281             case 'K':      /* The header must start at least 1 byte back  */
00282                 bp -= 1; break;
00283             case 'P':      /* Either this is the header, or the header must
00284                             * start at least 4  back
00285                             */
00286                 if (bp[1] == 'K' && bp[2] == 5 && bp[3] == 6) {
00287                     int endpos = position + (bp - buffer);
00288                     if (endpos + ENDHDRSIZ + ENDCOM(bp) == length) {
00289                         unsigned long cenpos = endpos - ENDSIZ(bp);
00290                         unsigned long locpos = cenpos - ENDOFF(bp);
00291                         entry->jar.cenpos = cenpos;
00292                         entry->jar.locpos = locpos;
00293                         result = true;
00294                         goto done;
00295                     }
00296                 }
00297             /* FALL THROUGH */
00298             default:      /* This char isn't in the header signature, so
00299                            * the header must start at least four chars back */
00300                 bp -= 4;
00301         }
00302 
00303         if (bp < buffer) {
00304             /* We've moved outside our window into the file.  We must
00305              * move the window backwards */
00306             int count = position - minPosition; /* Bytes left in file */
00307             if (count == 0) {
00308                 /* Nothing left to read.  Time to give up */
00309                 goto done;
00310             } else {
00311                 /* up to ((bp - buffer) + ENDHDRSIZ) bytes in the buffer might
00312                  * still be part of the end header, so the most bytes we can
00313                  * actually read are
00314                  *      bufferSize - ((bp - buffer) + ENDHDRSIZE).
00315                  */
00316                 int available = (bufferSize - ENDHDRSIZ) + (buffer - bp);
00317                 if (count > available) {
00318                     count = available;
00319                 }
00320             }
00321             /* Back up, while keeping our virtual position the same */
00322             position -= count;
00323             bp += count;
00324             memmove(buffer + count, buffer, bufferSize - count);
00325             if (   (fseek(file, position, SEEK_SET) < 0)
00326               || (fread(buffer, sizeof(char), count, file) != (unsigned)count)) {
00327                 goto done;
00328             }
00329         }
00330     } /* end of for loop */
00331 
00332  done:
00333     if (file != NULL) {
00334         fclose(file);
00335     }
00336     return result;
00337 }
00338 
00339 /*=========================================================================
00340  * FUNCTION:      jarCRC32
00341  * OVERVIEW:      Returns the CRC of an array of bytes, using the same
00342  *                algorithm as used by the JAR reader.
00343  * INTERFACE:
00344  *   parameters:  data:     pointer to the array of bytes
00345  *                length:   length of data, in bytes
00346  *   returns:     CRC
00347  *=======================================================================*/
00348 
00349 static unsigned long
00350 jarCRC32(unsigned char *data, unsigned long length) {
00351     unsigned long crc = 0xFFFFFFFF;
00352     unsigned int j;
00353     for ( ; length > 0; length--, data++) {
00354         crc ^= *data;
00355         for (j = 8; j > 0; --j) {
00356         crc = (crc & 1) ? ((crc >> 1) ^ 0xedb88320) : (crc >> 1);
00357         }
00358     }
00359     return ~crc;
00360 }
00361 
00362 /*=========================================================================
00363  * FUNCTION:      loadJARfile()
00364  * TYPE:          load JAR file
00365  * OVERVIEW:      Internal function used by openClassfileInternal().
00366  *
00367  *  This function reads the specified class file from the JAR file.  The
00368  *  result is returned as a JAR_DataStream*.  NULL is returned if it
00369  *  cannot find the file, or there is some error.
00370  *
00371  * INTERFACE:
00372  *   parameters:  entry:    zip file entry for the JAR file
00373  *                filename: class file name to search for
00374  *   returns:     JAR_DataStream* for saving the JAR file info, or NULL.
00375  *=======================================================================*/
00376 
00377 JAR_DataStreamPtr
00378 loadJARfile(zip_t *entry, const char* filename)
00379 {
00380     JAR_DataStreamPtr jdstream = NULL; /* result on error */
00381     unsigned int filenameLength;
00382     unsigned int nameLength;
00383     char buff[BUFSIZ];
00384     char *UTFfilename = &buff[0];
00385     char *p = str_buffer;   /* temporary storage */
00386     int offset;
00387     char *fname = NULL;
00388     FILE *file = fopen(entry->name, "rb");
00389     unsigned long decompLen = 0; /* the decompressed length */
00390     unsigned long compLen; /* the compressed length */
00391     unsigned long method; /* how it is stored */
00392     unsigned long expectedCRC; /* expected CRC */
00393     unsigned long actualCRC;
00394 
00395     unsigned char *decompData = NULL;
00396     if (file == NULL) {
00397         goto done;
00398     }
00399 
00400     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00401         jio_fprintf(stderr,
00402                "loadJARfile: Opening zip file %s to search for [%s]\n",
00403                 entry->name, filename);
00404 
00405     /* add the .class to the filename */
00406 
00407     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00408         jio_fprintf(stderr,
00409         "loadJARfile: Adding '.class' to %s size [%d]\n", filename, strlen(filename));
00410 
00411     /* Conversion for Japanese filenames */
00412     native2utf8(filename, UTFfilename, BUFSIZ);
00413 
00414     /* allocate fname large enough to hold .class + '\0' terminator */
00415     fname = (char *)malloc(strlen(UTFfilename) + 6 + 1);
00416     sprintf(fname, "%s.class", UTFfilename);
00417     filenameLength=strlen(fname);
00418     fname[filenameLength]='\0';
00419 
00420     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00421         jio_fprintf(stderr,
00422         "loadJARfile: Searching for filename [%s]\n", fname);
00423 
00424     /* Go to the start of the central headers */
00425     offset = entry->jar.cenpos;
00426     for (;;) {
00427 
00428         if (/* Go to the next central header */
00429             (fseek(file, offset, SEEK_SET) < 0)
00430             /* Read the bytes */
00431         || (fread(p, sizeof(char), CENHDRSIZ, file) != CENHDRSIZ)
00432             /* Make sure it is a header */
00433         || (GETSIG(p) != CENSIG)) {
00434             goto done;
00435         }
00436 
00437         /* Get the nameLength */
00438         nameLength = CENNAM(p);
00439 
00440         if (nameLength == filenameLength) {
00441             if (fread(p + CENHDRSIZ, sizeof(char), nameLength, file)
00442                      != nameLength) {
00443                 goto done;
00444             }
00445             if (memcmp(p + CENHDRSIZ, fname, nameLength) == 0) {
00446 
00447                 if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00448                     jio_fprintf(stderr, "loadJARfile: Class name found [%s]...\n", fname);
00449                 break;
00450             }
00451         }
00452 
00453         /* Set offset to the next central header */
00454         offset += CENHDRSIZ + nameLength + CENEXT(p) + CENCOM(p);
00455 
00456     } /* end loop */
00457 
00458     /* p points at the central header for the file */
00459     decompLen = CENLEN(p); /* the decompressed length */
00460     compLen   = CENSIZ(p); /* the compressed length */
00461     method    = CENHOW(p); /* how it is stored */
00462     expectedCRC = CENCRC(p); /* expected CRC */
00463     
00464     /* Make sure file is not encrypted */
00465     if ((CENFLG(p) & 1) == 1) {
00466         fprintf(stderr, "Entry is encrypted");
00467         goto done;
00468     }
00469 
00470     jdstream =
00471         (JAR_DataStreamPtr)sysMalloc(sizeof(JAR_DataStream) + decompLen);
00472     decompData = (unsigned char *)(jdstream + 1);
00473 
00474     if (/* Go to the beginning of the LOC header */
00475            (fseek(file, entry->jar.locpos + CENOFF(p), SEEK_SET) < 0)
00476         /* Read it */
00477         || (fread(p, sizeof(char), LOCHDRSIZ, file) != LOCHDRSIZ)
00478         /* Skip over name and extension, if any */
00479         || (fseek(file, LOCNAM(p) + LOCEXT(p), SEEK_CUR) < 0)) {
00480         goto done;
00481     }
00482 
00483     switch (method) {
00484         case STORED:
00485             if (compLen != decompLen) {
00486                 return NULL;
00487             }
00488             fread(decompData, sizeof(char), decompLen, file);
00489             break;
00490 
00491         case DEFLATED: {
00492             bool inflateOK;
00493             inflateOK = inflate(file, compLen, decompData, decompLen);
00494 
00495             if (!inflateOK) {
00496                 sysFree(jdstream);
00497                 jdstream = NULL;
00498             }
00499             break;
00500         }
00501 
00502         default:
00503             sysFree(jdstream);
00504             jdstream = NULL;
00505         break;
00506     }
00507 
00508     actualCRC = jarCRC32(decompData, decompLen);
00509     if (actualCRC != expectedCRC) {
00510         printf("Unexpected CRC value");
00511     }
00512 
00513     done:
00514     if (file != NULL) {
00515         fclose(file);
00516     }
00517 
00518     if (fname != NULL) {
00519         sysFree(fname);
00520     }
00521 
00522     if (jdstream != NULL) {
00523 
00524         jdstream->type = JAR_RESOURCE;
00525         jdstream->data = decompData;
00526         jdstream->dataLen = decompLen;
00527         jdstream->dataIndex = 0;
00528         jdstream->mode = JAR_READ;
00529     }
00530     return jdstream;
00531 }
00532 
00533 /*=========================================================================
00534  * FUNCTION:      ReadFromZip()
00535  * TYPE:          Reads and verifies ZIP file entries
00536  * OVERVIEW:      Internal function used by ProcessInputs() and recurse_dir.
00537  *
00538  *  This function reads all the Zip entries, and stores them temporarily in
00539  *  tmpdir. If the ZIP entry read is a class file, then VerifyFile is invoked
00540  *  to verify the class file. Otherwise, the file read is simply copied over
00541  *  temporarily to the tmpdir. These are later used for generating a new JAR
00542  *  file.
00543  *
00544  * INTERFACE:
00545  *   parameters:  ZipEntry:  name of the zip file entry.
00546  *   returns:     nothing
00547  *=======================================================================*/
00548 
00549 void
00550 ReadFromZip(zip_t *entry)
00551 {
00552     unsigned int nameLength;
00553     char *filename = NULL;
00554     char *p = str_buffer;               /* temporary storage */
00555     int offset, nextOffset;
00556     int fd;
00557     int status;
00558     JAR_DataStreamPtr jdstream = NULL;
00559     struct stat stat_buf;
00560     unsigned char *decompData = NULL;
00561     unsigned long decompLen = 0;     /* the decompressed length */
00562 
00563     FILE *file = fopen(entry->name, "rb");
00564     if (file == NULL) {
00565         goto done;
00566     }
00567 
00568     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00569         jio_fprintf(stderr,
00570                "ReadFromZip: Opened zip file to read classes \n");
00571 
00572     /* initialize */
00573     memset(manifestfile, 0, 1024);
00574 
00575     /* Go to the start of the central headers */
00576     offset = entry->jar.cenpos;
00577     for (;;) {
00578 
00579         if (/* Go to the next central header */
00580            (fseek(file, offset, SEEK_SET) < 0)
00581         /* Read the bytes */
00582         || (fread(p, sizeof(char), CENHDRSIZ, file) != CENHDRSIZ)
00583         /* Make sure it is a header */
00584         || (GETSIG(p) != CENSIG)) {
00585             goto done;
00586         }
00587         /* Get the nameLength */
00588         nameLength = CENNAM(p);
00589 
00590         if (fread(p + CENHDRSIZ, sizeof(char), nameLength, file)
00591             != nameLength) {
00592             goto done;
00593         }
00594 
00595         /* initialize the filename with nulls every time */
00596 
00597         filename = (char *) sysCalloc(STRINGBUFFERSIZE, nameLength);
00598 
00599         if (filename == NULL) {
00600             fprintf(stderr, "ReadFromZip: Out of memory \n");
00601             exit(1);
00602         }
00603 
00604         memcpy(filename, p + CENHDRSIZ, nameLength);
00605 
00606         /* We have to calculate nextOffset now, because VerifyFile bashes
00607          * str_buffer
00608          */
00609         nextOffset = offset + CENHDRSIZ + nameLength + CENEXT(p) + CENCOM(p);
00610 
00611         if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00612             jio_fprintf(stderr,
00613                "ReadFromZip: filename read %s\n", filename);
00614 
00615         /* extract the .class from the filename */
00616         if (nameLength > 6 &&
00617             strcmp(filename + nameLength - 6, ".class") == 0) {
00618             /* Verify the class file */
00619 
00620             if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00621                 jio_fprintf(stderr,
00622                  "ReadFromZip: Extracted '.class' from %s\n", filename);
00623 
00624             filename[nameLength-6] = 0;
00625 
00626             if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00627                 jio_fprintf(stderr,
00628                  "ReadFromZip: Verifying classfile %s\n", filename);
00629 
00630             /* call VerifyFile to verify the class */
00631             VerifyFile(filename);
00632         } else {
00633             /* Read and copy over the file to tmpdir */
00634             /* p points at the central header for the file */
00635             unsigned long compLen   = CENSIZ(p); /* the compressed length */
00636             unsigned long method    = CENHOW(p); /* how it is stored */
00637             unsigned long expectedCRC = CENCRC(p); /* expected CRC */
00638             unsigned long actualCRC;
00639 
00640             decompLen = CENLEN(p);
00641 
00642             if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00643                 jio_fprintf(stderr,
00644                   "READFROMZIP: Reading file [%s]...\n", filename);
00645 
00646             /* Make sure file is not encrypted */
00647             if ((CENFLG(p) & 1) == 1) {
00648                 jio_fprintf(stderr, "Entry is encrypted\n");
00649                 goto done;
00650             }
00651 
00652             jdstream =
00653                 (JAR_DataStreamPtr)sysMalloc(sizeof(JAR_DataStream) + decompLen);
00654             decompData = (unsigned char *)(jdstream + 1);
00655 
00656             if (/* Go to the beginning of the LOC header */
00657                    (fseek(file, entry->jar.locpos + CENOFF(p), SEEK_SET) < 0)
00658                 /* Read it */
00659                 || (fread(p, sizeof(char), LOCHDRSIZ, file) != LOCHDRSIZ)
00660                 /* Skip over name and extension, if any */
00661                 || (fseek(file, LOCNAM(p) + LOCEXT(p), SEEK_CUR) < 0)) {
00662                 goto done;
00663             }
00664 
00665             switch (method) {
00666                 case STORED:
00667                     if (compLen != decompLen) {
00668                         sysFree(jdstream);
00669                         jdstream = NULL;
00670                         goto done;
00671                     }
00672                     fread(decompData, sizeof(char), decompLen, file);
00673                     break;
00674 
00675                 case DEFLATED: {
00676                     bool inflateOK;
00677                     inflateOK = inflate(file, compLen, decompData, decompLen);
00678 
00679                     if (!inflateOK) {
00680                         sysFree(jdstream);
00681                         jdstream = NULL;
00682                     }
00683                     break;
00684                 }
00685 
00686                 default:
00687                     sysFree(jdstream);
00688                     jdstream = NULL;
00689                 break;
00690             }
00691 
00692             actualCRC = jarCRC32(decompData, decompLen);
00693             if (actualCRC != expectedCRC) {
00694                 jio_fprintf(stderr, "Unexpected CRC value\n");
00695             }
00696 
00697             /* create the tempdir if it doesn't already exist */
00698             {
00699                 char fname[1024];     /* file name restored from JAR */
00700                 char *dir;
00701                 char *q;
00702                 char *sfname = fname; /* system-specific file name */
00703                 char *dname = fname;  /* destination file name */
00704 
00705                 if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00706                     jio_fprintf(stderr,
00707                         "Reading filename [%s] from JAR \n", filename);
00708 
00709                 sprintf(fname, "%s%c%s", tmp_dir,
00710                                          (char) LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR,
00711                                          filename);
00712 
00713                 if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00714                     jio_fprintf(stderr,
00715                                "Before conversion: fname [%s]  sfname [%s]\n",
00716                                 fname, sfname);
00717 
00718         /*
00719                  * convert JAR name to the system-specific file name
00720                  */
00721 
00722                 sfname = JARname2fname(fname, dname, strlen(fname)+1);
00723 
00724                 if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00725                     jio_fprintf(stderr,
00726                                 "After conversion: Converted [%s] to [%s]\n",
00727                                  fname, sfname);
00728 
00729                 if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00730                     jio_fprintf(stderr,
00731                       "Preparing to write file [%s]\n", sfname);
00732 
00733                 dir = strdup(sfname);
00734                 q = strrchr(dir, (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR);
00735                 if (q) {
00736                     *q = 0;
00737                     ensure_tmpdir_exists(dir);
00738                 }
00739                 free(dir);
00740 
00741                 /* Attempt to stat or open only if this is NOT a directory */
00742 
00743                 if (!(sfname[strlen(sfname)-1] == (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR)) {
00744                     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00745                         jio_fprintf(stderr,
00746                         "Attempting stat on dir [%s]\n", sfname);
00747 
00748                     status = stat(sfname, &stat_buf);
00749 
00750                     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00751                         jio_fprintf(stderr,
00752                         "Status from stat of [%s] is %d\n", sfname, status);
00753 
00754                     /* Attempt to open only if the file does not already exist.
00755                      * This is indicated by the stat command returning -1.
00756                      * And this is not a directory.
00757                      */
00758 
00759                     if ((status < 0) || !(stat_buf.st_mode & S_IFDIR)) {
00760 
00761                         if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00762                             jio_fprintf(stderr,
00763                                 "Opening file [%s]\n", sfname);
00764                         fd = open(sfname, O_WRONLY | O_CREAT | O_TRUNC , 0644);
00765 
00766 
00767                         if (fd < 0) {
00768                             panic("failed to open %s", sfname);
00769                         }
00770 
00771                         if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00772                             jio_fprintf(stderr,
00773                             "Writing file [%s]...\n", sfname);
00774 
00775                         /* write the file to the tmpdir just created */
00776                         write(fd, decompData, decompLen);
00777 
00778                         /* check for the JAR Manifest.mf file */
00779 
00780                         if (isManifestfile(sfname, strlen(sfname))) {
00781                 /* save it for using it later to create
00782                              * the JAR file
00783                              */
00784                             memcpy(manifestfile, sfname + strlen(tmp_dir), strlen(sfname));
00785 
00786                             if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00787                                 jio_fprintf(stderr,
00788                                             "Saving JAR manifest file [%s]\n",
00789                                             manifestfile);
00790                         }
00791 
00792                         close(fd);
00793                     }
00794                 }
00795             }
00796             if (jdstream != NULL) {
00797                 sysFree(jdstream);
00798                 jdstream = NULL;
00799             }
00800         }
00801 
00802         /* Set offset to the next central header */
00803         offset = nextOffset;
00804         sysFree(filename);
00805         filename = NULL;
00806     }
00807 done:
00808 
00809     if (file != NULL) {
00810         fclose(file);
00811     }
00812 
00813     if (jdstream != NULL) {
00814 
00815         jdstream->type = JAR_RESOURCE;
00816         jdstream->data = decompData;
00817         jdstream->dataLen = decompLen;
00818         jdstream->dataIndex = 0;
00819         jdstream->mode = JAR_READ;
00820     }
00821 
00822     if (filename != NULL)
00823         sysFree(filename);
00824 }
00825 
00826 /*=========================================================================
00827  * FUNCTION:      remove_dir()
00828  * TYPE:          Handles removing files from recursive directories
00829  * OVERVIEW:      Internal function called by ProcessJARfile().
00830  *
00831  *  This function reads a directory, searching for either another directory,
00832  *  or an individual class name that is to be removed using the remove()
00833  *  API call.
00834  *
00835  * INTERFACE:
00836  *   parameters:  dirname   name of the directory entry.
00837  *                pkgname   name of the package
00838  *   returns:     nothing
00839  *=======================================================================*/
00840 static void remove_dir(char *dirname, char *pkgname)
00841 {
00842     struct dirent *ent;
00843     char buf[MAXPACKAGENAME];
00844     char pkgbuf[MAXPACKAGENAME];
00845     char tmpbuf[MAXPACKAGENAME];
00846     char tmppkg[MAXPACKAGENAME];
00847     char tmppkgbuf[MAXPACKAGENAME];
00848     char *name = NULL;
00849     DIR *dir = opendir(dirname);
00850     int err = 0;
00851     int status;
00852 
00853     /* Initialize the buffers to 0 */
00854     memset(buf, 0, sizeof(buf));
00855     memset(pkgbuf, 0, sizeof(pkgbuf));
00856     memset(tmpbuf, 0, sizeof(tmpbuf));
00857     memset(tmppkg, 0, sizeof(tmppkg));
00858     memset(tmppkgbuf, 0, sizeof(tmppkgbuf));
00859 
00860     if (dir == NULL) {
00861         fprintf(stderr, "Can't open dir %s\n", dirname);
00862         exit(1);
00863     }
00864     for (ent = readdir(dir); ent; ent = readdir(dir)) {
00865         struct stat stat_buf;
00866         int len;
00867         name = ent->d_name;
00868 
00869         if (strcmp(name, ".") == 0 || strcmp(name, "..") == 0) {
00870             continue;
00871         }
00872 
00873         strcpy(pkgbuf, pkgname);
00874         if (pkgname[0] != 0) {
00875             /* concatenate '/' to the package name */
00876             sprintf(tmppkgbuf, "%s%c", pkgbuf, (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR);
00877         }
00878         if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00879             jio_fprintf(stderr,
00880                "remove_dir: Reading filename [%s] from directory [%s]\n",
00881                         name, dirname);
00882 
00883         /* we just have a class file that needs to be removed */
00884 
00885         len = strlen(name);
00886 
00887         /* append the dirname and name */
00888         strcpy(buf, dirname);
00889         strcat(buf, name);
00890 
00891         status = stat(buf, &stat_buf);
00892 
00893         if ((status == 0) && !(stat_buf.st_mode & S_IFDIR)) {
00894             /* remove if this is a file and not a directory */
00895 
00896             if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00897                     jio_fprintf(stderr,
00898                 "remove_dir: Removing file [%s] \n", buf);
00899 
00900             err = remove(buf);
00901 
00902             if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose) {
00903                 jio_fprintf(stderr,
00904                            "remove_dir: remove() returned error %d\n", err);
00905             }
00906 
00907         } else {
00908             strcat(tmppkgbuf, name);
00909             stat(buf, &stat_buf);
00910             len = strlen(buf);
00911 
00912             if (stat_buf.st_mode & S_IFDIR) {
00913                 /* handle the recursive directory found */
00914 
00915                 sprintf(tmpbuf, "%s%c", buf, (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR);
00916 
00917                 if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00918                     jio_fprintf(stderr,
00919                      "remove_dir: Recursive dir, calling remove_dir [%s,%s]\n",
00920                       tmpbuf, tmppkgbuf);
00921                 remove_dir(tmpbuf, tmppkgbuf);
00922                 continue;
00923             }
00924         }
00925 
00926     }
00927 
00928     /* close the directory to free the dirp first */
00929     closedir(dir);
00930 
00931     /* Remove the directory by calling rmdir() or remove() API as appropriate */
00932 
00933     sprintf(tmppkg,"%s", dirname);
00934 
00935     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00936         jio_fprintf(stderr,
00937                   "remove_dir: Removing [%s]\n", tmppkg);
00938 
00939     err = remove (tmppkg);
00940 
00941     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose) {
00942         if (err != 0) {
00943             jio_fprintf(stderr,
00944                      "remove_dir: remove(%s) failed with error %d\n",
00945                        tmppkg, err);
00946         }
00947     }
00948 
00949 }
00950 /*=========================================================================
00951  * FUNCTION:      ProcessJARfile()
00952  * TYPE:          Processes ZIP file entries
00953  * OVERVIEW:      Internal function called by ProcessInputs().
00954  *
00955  *  This function processes a JAR file by first creating a zip entry for
00956  *  reading JAR directories, then calls ReadFromZip() to read the Zip
00957  *  class names and verifies them. It finally creates a new JAR file and
00958  *  places all the verified classes into it.
00959  *  It returns a boolean type to indicate if a valid JAR file was found
00960  *  and the contents of the JAR file were processed without errors.
00961  *
00962  * INTERFACE:
00963  *   parameters:  buf:  JAR file name.
00964  *                len:  size of the file
00965  *   returns:     boolean type
00966  *=======================================================================*/
00967 bool ProcessJARfile(char *buf, int len)  {
00968 
00969     zip_t *zipEntry;
00970     struct stat stat_buf;
00971     char *fname = NULL;
00972     char *buffer = NULL;
00973     char *jarName = NULL;
00974     char dirdelim = '\0';        /* directory delimiter */
00975     int err = 0;
00976     int tmpdir_len = 0;
00977     int statcode;
00978     char tmpdir_buf[MAXPACKAGENAME];
00979 
00980     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00981         jio_fprintf(stderr,
00982                "ProcessJARfile: JAR file [%s] Size [%d]\n", buf, len);
00983 
00984     statcode = stat(buf, &stat_buf);
00985 
00986     /* Create the zip entry for searching the JAR directories.
00987      * If the zip entry is NULL, it would indicate that we ran
00988      * out of memory and would have exited already.
00989      */
00990 
00991     zipEntry = getZipEntry (buf, len);
00992 
00993     if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
00994         jio_fprintf(stderr, "Searching for JAR directories...\n");
00995 
00996     /* search for the JAR directories */
00997     if (findJARDirectories(zipEntry, &stat_buf)) {
00998               /* the JAR directories were found */
00999 
01000         JARfile = true;
01001         zipFileName = buf;
01002         if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose) {
01003             jio_fprintf(stderr,
01004                         "ProcessJARfile: JAR directory [%s] found \n",
01005                          zipEntry->name);
01006         }
01007 
01008         zipEntry->type = 'j';
01009 
01010         /* Read and Verify the classes from the ZIP file */
01011 
01012        if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
01013            jio_fprintf(stderr, "ProcessJARfile: Verifying Zip class names...\n");
01014 
01015        pushJarFileOntoClassPath(zipEntry);
01016 
01017        ReadFromZip(zipEntry);
01018 
01019        popClassPath();
01020 
01021        /* Ensure that the output_dir also exists or create it if it
01022         * does not already exist
01023         */
01024 
01025        if (output_dir != NULL) {
01026            char *dir = strdup(output_dir);
01027 
01028            if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
01029                jio_fprintf(stderr, "ProcessJARfile: Checking if output [%s] exists\n",
01030                    output_dir);
01031            ensure_dir_exists(dir);
01032            ensure_dir_writable(dir);
01033            free(dir);
01034        }
01035 
01036        /* Create a JAR file only if the input parameter was a JAR file,
01037         * the tmp_dir was created with classes verified and an output
01038         * dir also exists.
01039         */
01040 
01041        if (JARfile && tmpDirExists && output_dir) {
01042            const char *p;
01043 
01044            /* Allocate enough space to hold the JAR name */
01045            jarName = (char *)sysCalloc(len+32, len);
01046 
01047            if (jarName == NULL) {
01048                fprintf(stderr, "ProcessJARfile: Out of memory");
01049                exit(1);
01050            }
01051 
01052            if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
01053                jio_fprintf(stderr, "ProcessJARfile: Creating JAR file of verified classes...\n");
01054            /* search for the last '/' to get the actual JAR file name */
01055            for (p = buf+len; ;) {
01056                --p;
01057 
01058                if (*p == '/' || *p == '\\') {
01059                    dirdelim = *p;
01060                    memcpy(jarName, p+1, (len-1)-(p-buf));
01061                    if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
01062                        jio_fprintf(stderr, "ProcessJARfile: JAR file [%s]\n", jarName);
01063                    break;
01064                }
01065                if (p == buf) {
01066                    /* no directories in path, get the individual JAR name */
01067                    strncpy(jarName, buf, len);
01068                    if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
01069                        jio_fprintf(stderr, "ProcessJARfile: JAR filename [%s]\n", jarName);
01070                    break;
01071                }
01072            }
01073 
01074            /* move the verified classes into a JAR file */
01075 
01076            /* Be sure to allocate enough space to hold the sprintfs below */
01077            fname = (char *)malloc(strlen(output_dir)+ len+32);
01078 
01079            if (fname == NULL) {
01080                fprintf(stderr, "ProcessJARfile: Out of memory");
01081                exit(1);
01082            }
01083 
01084            if (dirdelim != '\0') {
01085                sprintf(fname, "%s%c%s", output_dir,
01086                dirdelim, jarName);
01087            } else {
01088                sprintf(fname, "%s%c%s", output_dir,
01089                        (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR, jarName);
01090            }
01091 
01092            /* Be sure to allocate enough space to hold the sprintfs below */
01093            /* The size here must be adjusted anytime the buffer used in the
01094             * sprintfs is extended.
01095             */
01096 
01097            buffer = (char *)malloc(strlen(output_dir)+strlen(fname) +
01098                     strlen(tmp_dir) + strlen(manifestfile) +
01099                     strlen(tmp_dir) + 51);
01100            if (buffer == NULL) {
01101                fprintf(stderr, "ProcessJARfile: Out of memory");
01102                exit(1);
01103            }
01104 
01105            if (verbose) {
01106                    if (isManifestfile(manifestfile, strlen(manifestfile))) {
01107                        /* use existing manifest if one exists */
01108                        sprintf(buffer, "jar -cvfm \"%s\" %s%c%s -C %s .",
01109                                        fname, tmp_dir,
01110                                        (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR,
01111                                        manifestfile, tmp_dir);
01112 
01113                    } else {
01114 
01115                sprintf(buffer, "jar -cvfM \"%s\" -C %s .",
01116                                         fname, tmp_dir);
01117 
01118                    }
01119            } else {
01120                /* Run jar in non-verbose mode, and log errors in a file */
01121 
01122                    if (isManifestfile(manifestfile, strlen(manifestfile))) {
01123                        /* use existing manifest if one exists */
01124 
01125                        /* create JAR with existing manifest file */
01126                        /* Redirect errors and stdout to jarlog.txt */
01127                         sprintf(buffer,
01128                           "sh -c \"jar -cfm \\\"%s\\\" %s%c%s -C %s .\" > \"%s%c\"%s",
01129                           fname, tmp_dir, (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR,
01130                           manifestfile, tmp_dir, output_dir,
01131                           (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR,"jarlog.txt 2>&1");
01132                    } else {
01133                        /* create JAR with no manifest since none exists */
01134                        /* Redirect errors and stdout to jarlog.txt */
01135                        sprintf(buffer,
01136                            "sh -c \"jar -cfM \\\"%s\\\" -C %s .\" > \"%s%c\"%s",
01137                            fname, tmp_dir, output_dir,
01138                            (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR,"jarlog.txt 2>&1");
01139 
01140                    }
01141            }
01142            if (verbose) {
01143                jio_fprintf(stderr, "Executing command [%s]\n", buffer);
01144            }
01145 
01146            err = system(buffer);
01147 
01148            if (err != 0) {
01149                /* jar file creation failed - return back the error */
01150                fprintf(stderr, "JAR file creation failed with error %d\n", err);
01151                if (!verbose)
01152                    fprintf(stderr,
01153 "The preverified classes if any are in %s. See jar log of errors in %s%c%s \n",
01154                   tmp_dir, output_dir, (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR, "jarlog.txt");
01155            } else {
01156                if (!verbose) {
01157                    /* remove the jar log file if no error occurred */
01158                    char *jarfn = NULL;
01159                    int error;
01160 
01161                    jarfn = (char *)malloc(strlen(output_dir)+10+1+1);
01162 
01163                    if (jarfn == NULL) {
01164                        fprintf(stderr, "ProcessJARfile: Out of memory");
01165                            exit(1);
01166                    }
01167 
01168                    sprintf(jarfn, "%s%c%s", output_dir,
01169                            (char)LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR, "jarlog.txt");
01170                    error = remove(jarfn);    /* remove the file */
01171 
01172                    if (jarfn != NULL)
01173                        sysFree(jarfn);
01174                }
01175 
01176                /* prepare to remove the tmp directory */
01177                /* copy the tmp directory name to a buffer */
01178                strcpy(tmpdir_buf, tmp_dir);
01179                tmpdir_len = strlen(tmp_dir);
01180 
01181                /* Append dir separator if it does not yet exist */
01182                if (tmpdir_buf[tmpdir_len - 1] != LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR &&
01183                    tmpdir_buf[tmpdir_len - 1] != DIR_SEPARATOR) {
01184                    tmpdir_buf[tmpdir_len] = LOCAL_DIR_SEPARATOR;
01185                    tmpdir_buf[tmpdir_len + 1] = 0;
01186                }
01187 
01188                /* remove the tmp_dir and all its contents recursively */
01189                remove_dir(tmpdir_buf, "");
01190             } /* jar creation returned no error */
01191         }
01192     } else {
01193         if (JAR_DEBUG && verbose)
01194             jio_fprintf(stderr, "JAR directories not found for JAR file [%s]\n", buf);
01195         if (fname != NULL)
01196             sysFree(fname);
01197         if (zipEntry != NULL)
01198             sysFree(zipEntry);
01199         return false; /* could not locate JAR directories - invalid JAR file */
01200     }
01201 
01202     if (fname != NULL)
01203         sysFree(fname);
01204 
01205     if (zipEntry != NULL)
01206         sysFree(zipEntry);
01207 
01208     if (buffer != NULL)
01209         sysFree(buffer);
01210 
01211     if (jarName != NULL)
01212         sysFree(jarName);
01213 
01214     if ( err!=0 ) 
01215         exit(errorCode = 1);
01216 
01217     return JARfile;
01218 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:28 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
